---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(rstan)

coin_df <- read_csv("flips.csv")
```

```{r}
coin_df %>%
    mutate(
        Outcome = factor(
            ifelse(Heads == 1, "Heads", "Tails"),
            levels = c("Tails", "Heads")
        ),
        Stream = paste0("Stream ", Stream)
    ) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = Stream, fill = Outcome)) +
    geom_bar() +
    theme_classic() +
    labs(title = "Heads/Tails Distribution Over 2 Streams", x = "Stream", y = "Outcome Distribution")
```


```{r}
coin_data <- list(
  y1 = sum(coin_df$Heads[coin_df$Stream == 1]),
  n1 = sum(coin_df$Stream == 1),
  y2 = sum(coin_df$Heads[coin_df$Stream == 2]),
  n2 = sum(coin_df$Stream == 2),
  alpha1 = 90,
  alpha2 = 90,
  beta1 = 10,
  beta2 = 10
)

start_time <- Sys.time()
coin_fit <- stan(
  file   = "coin_model.stan",
  data   = coin_data,
  chains = 8,
  iter   = 5000,
  warmup = 1000
)
end_time <- Sys.time()
print(end_time - start_time)

print(coin_fit, pars = c("theta1", "theta2"))

coin_samples <- extract(coin_fit)

print(paste("Posterior mean theta1:", mean(coin_samples$theta1)))
print(paste("Posterior mean theta2:", mean(coin_samples$theta2)))

cat("95% CI theta1:", quantile(coin_samples$theta1, c(0.025, 0.975)), "\n")
cat("95% CI theta2:", quantile(coin_samples$theta2, c(0.025, 0.975)), "\n")
```

```{r}
traceplot(coin_fit)
```

```{r}
coin_ci_df <- data.frame(
  param = c("theta1", "theta2"),
  mean  = c(mean(samples$theta1), mean(samples$theta2)),
  lower = c(quantile(samples$theta1, 0.025), quantile(samples$theta2, 0.025)),
  upper = c(quantile(samples$theta1, 0.975), quantile(samples$theta2, 0.975))
)
```

```{r}
# Define the Parameters & Data
alpha_prior <- 90
beta_prior <- 10

# Data from the streams
y1 <- sum(coin_df$Heads[coin_df$Stream == 1])
n1 <- sum(coin_df$Stream == 1)

# Define the Log-Posterior Function
log_posterior_fn <- function(theta1) {
  log_likelihood <- dbinom(x = y1, size = n1, prob = theta1, log = TRUE)
  log_prior <- dbeta(x = theta1, shape1 = alpha_prior, shape2 = beta_prior, log = TRUE)
  log_posterior <- log_likelihood + log_prior
  return(log_posterior)
}

start_time <- Sys.time()

# Run the optimization
initial_guess <- 0.5 
fit <- optim(par = initial_guess, 
             fn = log_posterior_fn, 
             method = "L-BFGS-B",  # Use the bounded optimizer
             lower = 0.0001,       # Prevent testing exactly 0
             upper = 0.9999,       # Prevent testing exactly 1
             control = list(fnscale = -1),
             hessian = TRUE)

# Negate the Hessian to find Variance
theta_map <- fit$par

# We negate it (-H) and invert it to get the variance.
laplace_var <- solve(-fit$hessian)
laplace_sd <- sqrt(as.numeric(laplace_var))

end_time <- Sys.time()
print(end_time - start_time)

# Print the final Laplace Approximation stats
print(paste("Mode (Theta 1 MAP): ", theta_map))
# print(paste("Variance: ", as.numeric(laplace_var)))
# print(paste("Standard Deviation: ", laplace_sd))
# Calculate the 95% Credible Interval
ci_lower <- qnorm(0.025, mean = theta_map, sd = laplace_sd)
ci_upper <- qnorm(0.975, mean = theta_map, sd = laplace_sd)
print(paste("95% CI: ", ci_lower, ",", ci_upper))
```


```{r}
# Integrated Squared Error
theta1_hmc <- coin_samples$theta1
theta2_hmc <- coin_samples$theta2

mu1 <- 0.8283
sd1 <- sqrt(0.000718)

mu2 <- 0.8535
sd2 <- sqrt(0.000631)

# x-grid base on HMC
dens1 <- density(theta1_hmc, n = 4096)
dens2 <- density(theta2_hmc, n = 4096)

# Calculate the Laplace y-coordinates at the exact same x-grid
laplace_y1 <- dnorm(dens1$x, mean = mu1, sd = sd1)
laplace_y2 <- dnorm(dens2$x, mean = mu2, sd = sd2)

# Calculate the width of each tiny grid slice
dx1 <- dens1$x[2] - dens1$x[1]
dx2 <- dens2$x[2] - dens2$x[1]

# Integrate the squared difference
integrated_squared_error1 <- sum((dens1$y - laplace_y1)**2 * dx1)
integrated_squared_error2 <- sum((dens2$y - laplace_y2)**2 * dx2)

print(paste("Integrated Squared Error (theta 1): ", integrated_squared_error1))
print(paste("Integrated Squared Error (theta 2): ", integrated_squared_error2))
```


```{r}

ggplot(coin_ci_df, aes(x = mean, y = param)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper), width = 0.1) +
  xlim(0.73, 1)+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, aes())+
  theme_classic() +
  labs(title = "95% Posterior Credible Interval",
       y = "Parameter", x = "Value")

```

```{r}

pp_samples <- tibble(
  heads = rbinom(length(c(samples$theta1, samples$theta2)), 100, c(samples$theta1, samples$theta2))
)

laplace_mean <- mean(pp_samples$heads)
laplace_sd   <- sd(pp_samples$heads)

norm_df <- tibble(
  x = seq(min(pp_samples$heads), max(pp_samples$heads), length.out = 500),
  y = dnorm(x, mean = laplace_mean, sd = laplace_sd)
)

pp_samples %>%
  count(heads) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = heads, y = prop)) +
  geom_col(color = "steelblue", fill = "steelblue", size = 1.5) +
  geom_line(data = norm_df, aes(x = x, y = y), linetype = "solid", linewidth = 1, color = "red") +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    title = "Posterior Predictive: Heads in 100 Flips (Both Streams)",
    x = "Number of Heads",
    y = "Density"
  )

```

```{r}
cat_df <- data.frame(
  Hour = c(1, 2),
  Cat_Sighted = c(0, 1)
)

cat_df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Hour, y = Cat_Sighted)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#F8766D") +
  labs(
    title = "Cat-crossings Over 2 Hours", 
    x = "Hour",
    y = "Cat-crossings Observed"
    ) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(1, 2))
```



```{r}
# The average number of cats crossing the Grand every hour
# Measure the observed number of cat-crossings using a Poisson distribution

# Assume cat-crossings are rare, roughly 0.6 cat-crossings per hour
# Gamma distribution makes sense here since the possibility of cat-tsunami is not unheard of (long tail).
# Define the Parameters & Data
alpha_prior <- 1.2
beta_prior <- 0.2

# Watched for two hours (y1 and y2)
# Data from the streams
y1 <- 0  # 0 cats in stream 1
y2 <- 1  # 1 cat in stream 2

# Calculations for true density (for comparison visualization only)
alpha_post <- alpha_prior + 1 # (number of cats)
beta_post <- beta_prior + 2 # (number of hours)

# Define the Log-Posterior Function
log_posterior_fn <- function(lambda) {
  likelihood <- dpois(y1, lambda) * dpois(y2, lambda)
  prior <- dgamma(lambda, shape = alpha_prior, rate = beta_prior)
  posterior <- likelihood * prior
  return(log(posterior))
}


start_time <- Sys.time()
# Run the optimization
initial_guess <- 1
fit <- optim(par = initial_guess, 
             fn = log_posterior_fn, 
             method = "BFGS", 
             control = list(fnscale = -1),
             hessian = TRUE)

# Negate the Hessian to find Variance
lambda_map <- fit$par

# Since we maximized the log-posterior, fit$hessian is the true Hessian (H).
# We negate it (-H) and invert it to get the variance.
laplace_var <- solve(-fit$hessian)
laplace_sd <- sqrt(as.numeric(laplace_var))
end_time <- Sys.time()
print(end_time - start_time)

print(paste("Mode: ", lambda_map))
print(paste("Variance: ", as.numeric(laplace_var)))
print(paste("Standard Deviation: ", laplace_sd))
```

```{r}
cat_data <- list(
  y = cat_df$Cat_Sighted,
  n = length(cat_df$Cat_Sighted),
  alpha = 1.2,
  beta = 0.2
)

start_time <- Sys.time()
cat_fit <- stan(
  file   = "cat_crossing.stan",
  data   = cat_data,
  chains = 4,
  iter   = 5000,
  warmup = 1000
)
end_time <- Sys.time()
print(end_time - start_time)

print(cat_fit, pars = "lambda")

cat_samples <- extract(cat_fit)

print(paste("Posterior mean lambda:", mean(cat_samples$lambda)))

cat("95% CI lambda:", quantile(cat_samples$lambda, c(0.025, 0.975)), "\n")
```

```{r}
# Viz
x_vals <- seq(-1, 4, length.out = 500)

# Calculate the densities
true_density <- dgamma(x_vals, shape = alpha_post, rate = beta_post)
laplace_density <- dnorm(x_vals, mean = lambda_map, sd = laplace_sd)

# Plot the True Posterior
plot(x_vals, true_density, type = "l", lwd = 3, col = "blue",
     main = "Laplace Approx: 2 Streams (1 Cat Total)",
     xlab = "Rate of Cat Crossings (lambda)", ylab = "Density",
     xlim = c(-1, 3.5), ylim = c(0, max(true_density, laplace_density) + 0.1))

# Overlay the Laplace Approximation
lines(x_vals, laplace_density, lwd = 3, col = "red", lty = 2)

# Add a vertical line at zero to show the impossible domain
abline(v = 0, lwd = 2, lty = 3, col = "black")

# Print out the impossible negative probability mass
prob_negative <- pnorm(0, mean = lambda_map, sd = laplace_sd)
print(paste("Probability mass assigned to negative values: ", prob_negative * 100))
```
